content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN. In one operation, Chef can:


Choose any subarray [L,R][L, R][L,R] (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N)(1 \leq L \leq R \leq N)(1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N);
Add 111 to ALA_LAL‚Äã, subtract 111 from AL+1A_{L+1}AL+1‚Äã, add 111 to AL+2A_{L+2}AL+2‚Äã, subtract 111 from AL+3A_{L+3}AL+3‚Äã and so on, till ARA_RAR‚Äã.
Chef performed QQQ such operations where the ithi^{th}ith operation was performed on the subarray [Li,Ri][L_i, R_i][Li‚Äã,Ri‚Äã].

Determine the final **sum** of the array after these QQQ operations.

Note that a subarray is formed by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the beginning and some (possibly zero) elements from the end of the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers N,QN, QN,Q, number of elements and the number of queries.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2, \dots A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶AN‚Äã - denoting the array AAA.
The next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers with ithi^{th}ith line containing Li,RiL_i, R_iLi‚Äã,Ri‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output the final sum of the array after performing all the QQQ operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The queries are as follows:

Query 111: The chosen subarray is [1,3,4,4,2][1, 3, 4, 4, 2][1,3,4,4,2] which changes to [1+1,3‚àí1,4+1,4‚àí1,2+1]=[2,2,5,3,3][1+1, 3-1, 4+1, 4-1, 2+1] = [2, 2, 5, 3, 3][1+1,3‚àí1,4+1,4‚àí1,2+1]=[2,2,5,3,3]. Thus, the array becomes [2,2,5,3,3][2, 2, 5, 3, 3][2,2,5,3,3].
Query 222: The chosen subarray is [5,3][5, 3][5,3] which changes to [5+1,3‚àí1]=[6,2][5+1, 3-1] = [6, 2][5+1,3‚àí1]=[6,2]. Thus, the array becomes [2,2,6,2,3][2, 2, 6, 2, 3][2,2,6,2,3].
Query 333: The chosen subarray is [2][2][2] which changes to [2+1]=[3][2+1] = [3][2+1]=[3]. Thus, the array becomes [2,3,6,2,3][2, 3, 6, 2, 3][2,3,6,2,3].

The sum of final array is 2+3+6+2+3=162+3+6+2+3 = 162+3+6+2+3=16.
Test case 222: The queries are as follows:

Query 111: The chosen subarray is [4][4][4] which changes to [4+1]=[5][4+1] = [5][4+1]=[5]. Thus, the array becomes [5][5][5].
Query 222: The chosen subarray is [5][5][5] which changes to [5+1]=[6][5+1] = [6][5+1]=[6]. Thus, the array becomes [6][6][6].

The sum of final array is 666.
